## Introduction

On Oct 29, 2025, NVIDIA highlighted open-world foundation models for synthetic environments, aimed at accelerating robotics and autonomous system development.

NVIDIA introduced open-world foundation models that generate rich synthetic environments for 'physical AI'-robots, autonomous systems, and digital twins. The idea: train agents in vast, photorealistic worlds with controllable physics, materials, and edge cases-then transfer skills to the real world faster. Combined with Omniverse, OEMs can configure factories, test robot behaviors, and validate perception under rare scenarios. NVIDIA says this can cut engineering cycles dramatically by front-loading simulation and labeling. Expect early adopters in automotive and advanced manufacturing, where safety and reliability are mission-critical. If this approach holds, synthetic data will become a first-class input alongside real telemetry-shrinking the gap between virtual prototyping and production deployment.
